content:
This post is going to be part tutorial, part conversation, and part rant. If you have come here to know how to set up a blog using Hugo, please try not to get annoyed by the occasional rant. You have been warned.
I have always wanted to start a blog, despite having no idea what to write about. One fine day I decided to just start a blog and decide later about what to write. And then I did start a blog! But the content didn't come. Then I thought "Okay, I'll just write whatever I want, then maybe something will strike me". I wrote up 4-5 posts over time and published them! But after that happened, I abandoned the blog for months. Why? Because I still didn't know what to write.
Then I thought, why don't I complement my work with my blog? It would serve as a way for me to document my work while also possibly being helpful to you, my dear reader! So here I am, writing this post!
I am also planning to start a new series called Learn with me, in which I write about whatever I'm learning while I'm learning it. I decided to begin with the Learn with me - Multi-Armed Bandits series in which I'm going to explain Multi-Armed Bandits concepts in simple terms. I wish to be thorough in whatever I'm doing (except for writing blog posts maybe, else I will take forever if I keep rewriting until my post looks perfect 🤦‍♂️), so I'm going to take a lot of time to compose one post! If you're interested in MABs, do check out my posts!
The main reason I am writing this post is that setting up a blog with Hugo is a pain. Not because it's bad, but because it has so many features and I don't know which ones to use! I finally decided to end up following this configuration. Here's how you can create your own blog using Hugo and GitHub pages!
Publishing a website like mine I use Hugo, a static site generator written in Go, to create my blog. I write my blog posts in org-mode files in Emacs, which Hugo converts to HTML when I build the site. I prefer to use this workflow because
It is lightweight. It is minimalistic, yet has all the features I need. I am comfortable with the terminal and Emacs, so the workflow is easy for me. I'll walk you through the steps you can follow to set up a site that looks like mine.
Set up a hugo site Install Hugo. Since I use Manjaro, all I had to do was execute sudo pacman -S hugo. Create a new site with hugo new site my_blog. This creates a site folder called my_blog. Install the PaperMod theme using the following command (more detailed instructions are given here)
git clone https://github.com/adityatelange/hugo-PaperMod themes/PaperMod --depth=1 Copy the config from here and paste it into a file called config.yml. This file must be in your site folder. Customize and create content At this point your site should be ready to function. You can now:
Create a new post called "post.org" using the command hugo new post.org which creates that post and places it in contents/post.org. You can also create markdown files (many types of files are supported by Hugo). Serve your site using the hugo server to preview how your site would look. Do that by executing the following command from the root directory.
hugo serve -D Create as much content as you'd like! You can also customize the global site settings in the config.yml file. Hugo themes often contain lots of features that aren't enabled by default, which you can put to good use. If you don't like the theme, you can also install a new theme from the official Hugo themes repository. Just remember, if you decide to go on a quest to find the best Hugo theme out there, there is no guarantee that you will ever come back. Yet again, you have been warned!
Publish your site on Github Pages Create a Github repository called username.github.io, where username is your username. Install git, if you don't have it installed already! On Manjaro, just run sudo pacman -S git. From your root directory, run the following command (after replacing <username> with your username)
git clone https://github.com/<username>/<username>.github.io Rename the newly cloned repository. Call it "public"
mv <username>.github.io public Generate your site using the following command. This will create your html site in the public directory.
hugo Change the current working directory into public, then commit and push the site into Github.
git commit -a -m "First commit" git push -u origin main You should now see your site on https://<username>.github.io (replace <username> with your username). We're done! This is just the beginning though.
Additional Notes There are many ways you can make this workflow smoother so that you don't have to run git commit and git push over and over again. I personally use a deploy.sh zsh script to automate that process, which asks me for a commit message interactively before pushing. You can also use Makefiles to do that (if you prefer to use GNU make).
The following are the contents of deploy.sh (You can use this directly if you use zsh. Note that vared doesn't work in bash, so you should modify the script if you use bash.)
#!/bin/zsh vared -p "Enter commit message: " -c message printf "\e[33m\nBuilding project...\e[39m\n" hugo printf "\\e[33m\nPushing to pace577.github.io repository...\e[39m\n\n" cd public git add . git commit -m "$message" git push origin main printf "\e[32m\nSuccessfully deployed the website!\e[39m" To use this script, turn it into an executable and execute it.
chmod +x deploy.sh ./deploy.sh After creating the above script and making it into an executable, all you must do to publish your website is enter into your site directory and execute ./deploy.sh.
Some people might be going "Why go through this complicated process when you can instead push the entire Hugo site to GitHub and use CI/CD to deploy the directory you want to pages?" and those people would have a point. You can do that too, but I didn't want to do it since I didn't want my entire Hugo site on GitHub. I haven't done it, so I wouldn't know it. Feel free to check GitHub CI/CD out if you like!
You can also make the process of generating content easier using third-party software. Me being a regular Emacs user, I find the Emacs easy hugo page really useful in handling my workflow! Maybe I'll write up some posts on Emacs later 🤔.
Hope this helps. Cheers!
summary:
This post is going to be part tutorial, part conversation, and part rant. If you have come here to know how to set up a blog using Hugo, please try not to get annoyed by the occasional rant. You have been warned.
I have always wanted to start a blog, despite having no idea what to write about. One fine day I decided to just start a blog and decide later about what to write.
title: Setting up a blog with Hugo

content:
Growing up as a mathematics student taught me a thing or two about problem-solving, and most (if not all) math students love elegant solutions to problems. Back when I was 11th grade, the math teacher used to gave the entire class a small set of problems to solve, and discuss the solutions to these problems in the next class. The interesting thing was that he always discussed multiple ways to solve the same problem. Some were longer ways to do it, some were short, some were extremely short but weren't obvious, and solutions like these are what math folks tend to call elegant.
After all, who doesn't want short solutions to problems in life?
Experiences like this led me to believe that looking at a problem from multiple perspectives and finding different solutions from these perspectives is the key to mastering the art of problem-solving. And that's the reason setting out on a journey to become the best problem solver in the world is a bit disappointing; after coming up with an elegant solution to a difficult problem, you forget to feel happy and instead think "is there another way?".
I think that's what Duality is, in a sense: "different ways of looking at the same thing". Heads and tails, light and darkness, order and chaos, a set and its complement, all these are dualities. Tail on a coin is the absence of head, darkness is the absence of light, chaos is the absence of order, the complement of a set is everything else but the set. The dual of something, in this group of examples, is the absence of something, as it carries the same amount of information.
Vectors and linear functionals (Linear Algebra), time domain and frequency domain (Signal Processing), Kirchoff's junction law and loop law (Electrical Circuits) are also dualities. Notice the difference from the previous examples. Duality in this sense is not presence or absence of something, but rather, having alternate representations of the same information.
The above is my take on duality and I might be off track here, but what got me hooked on to the idea of duality is how we find "pairs" of similar representations. Why aren't there more similar representations? It seems obvious for the first set of examples above, but what about the second set? Are time domain and frequency domain representations the only two ways to represent a signal, or are there more? If not, can we somehow break down this second kind of duality (having alternate representations of the same thing) and represent it as the duality of the first kind (presence and absence of something)?
I think that's enough brain vomit for now since it's not particularly going anywhere. I came across the term today in my Applied Linear Algebra class and then watched two amazing videos by 3Blue1Brown on Duality in Linear algebra and Graph Duality. If you love math or love problem solving or love good animations or if you're addicted to YouTube, I highly recommend you check these videos out!
Until next time!
summary: Growing up as a mathematics student taught me a thing or two about problem-solving, and most (if not all) math students love elegant solutions to problems. Back when I was 11th grade, the math teacher used to gave the entire class a small set of problems to solve, and discuss the solutions to these problems in the next class. The interesting thing was that he always discussed multiple ways to solve the same problem.
title: Duality

content:
I get an average of 5 hours of sleep per day. Five hours.
It wasn't always like this. There were times when I slept around 8 hours a day. That's the recommended number of hours of sleep one must get right? I heard that different people tend to sleep different amounts of time, but I'm pretty sure that 5 hours is not in the set of all recommended durations of sleep for a human being. I know this because the consequences of less sleep are evident and visible. I shall not get into the details of what they are, because I would like to live in denial for some more time. Why? Because I like losing sleep.
Do you like losing sleep? If you think no, think again. I don't like losing sleep either, but that's because I want to reap the benefits of having a good sleep schedule. Who wouldn't want better focus, better immunity, better fitness, better mental health, better composure, lesser headaches, and oh, lesser hairfall? I do, but I also want the time that I get to use in exchange for my sleep time. Why?
Because I am in control of that time.
After spending the entire day fulfilling responsibilities, granting requests, doing tasks that you are supposed to do, wouldn't it be amazing to have some time to yourself? Time that you can spend any which way you want? After an exhausting day, I get very tempted to sacrifice sleep and watch an engaging thriller movie, or a sitcom to put my mind at ease. I tend to forget everything else when I'm watching these shows, and that's exactly what makes this experience so alluring. A sleepless night spent watching movies and tv shows gives me control over myself and gives me a break from my hectic life. I think I can be more precise.
Such a sleepless night gives me an illusion of control and a means to flee from reality.
The human mind is a pretty good negotiator. It ties up a beautiful story, promises a great time and manipulates your hormones so elegantly that you end up signing the sleepless night contract. But remember, never evaluate a contract by its promises, do it with its results. The next morning, you wake up with a feeling of dread. Heck, sometimes you don't even wake up and later regret not waking up early. You skip meals. You lose focus. You can't study properly. You don't ask doubts in class because you believe missed lots of what has been covered already, and that's the reason you don't understand anything. You think "Classes are getting difficult, I really need to study by myself to keep up". You think the same thing in every class and build up stress. You see an assignment and nothing makes sense to you. You get back home and lie on the bed and think "Damn, today was stressful". That's the cue for your inner negotiator to start talking.
"You seem stressed. Want to watch a movie and escape reality?"
And the cycle begins all over again, this time with even more stress. It makes your life more stressful, even miserable, but the cycle won't stop. You might ask "If it's such a bad thing, why do we keep doing it?". Because the mind doesn't care. What the mind cares about is comfort, and the dopamine that comes from watching TV shows gives a lot of comfort. It takes a lot of will to break a cycle driven by comfort, and it takes a little will to stay away from the cycle after you break away from it too. That's why it's so easy to fall back into the cycle, because a certain part of you wants to fall back into the cycle. That's the power comfort has on your mind.
So what should I do to stay away from this vicious cycle of sleeplessness? Simple. Stay away from comfort, whenever possible. And get used to staying uncomfortable.
Will it help you stay away from sacrificing sleep to watch movies? Yes. Will it help you to break or stay away from any habit that you don't want to have? Yes. Will you build will power? Yes, because being comfortable with staying uncomfortable is what willpower is (at least, that's what I think). Will it lead to a happy life? I have no clue. Is it even possible to get used to staying uncomfortable? I will try and let you know.
There ends my aimless but somewhat controlled chain of thought. Hope it helped!
I'm writing this for myself, to get a perspective, and for others who would like to know others' perspectives. I mix up "I"s and "you"s because it just feels good. If any of this troubles you, please stay away from it. Or get used to it, in which case you'll know that you have been inspired by my article.
summary:
I get an average of 5 hours of sleep per day. Five hours.
It wasn't always like this. There were times when I slept around 8 hours a day. That's the recommended number of hours of sleep one must get right? I heard that different people tend to sleep different amounts of time, but I'm pretty sure that 5 hours is not in the set of all recommended durations of sleep for a human being.
title: Sleep

content:
I don't think I take proper care of myself. It's not that I have good intentions, but somehow those intentions do not seem to translate into actions. I want to eat healthy food 3 times a day, drink plenty of water everyday, take 2 baths a day, read news everyday, learn something new everyday, talk to my parents everyday and do many more things! But I look at my lifestyle and I look at the list of things I want to do above, and I think to myself "How in the world can I do all that in a day?". As a student, I also need to attend classes, study outside classes, do assignments, prepare for exams, write exams and do things that improve myself everyday.
Can I really do all these things regularly? Just thinking about it scares me. Why? Because the order and discipline I need to have in my life in order to do all these things scares me. The solid structure that I would have in my life scares me. That's because I would like my life to be open to new things! I would like to be able to accommodate doing things that are not on my schedule once in a while. Maybe I want to hang out with my friends or read a book sometime, and I want to be able to do these things.
But now that I think about it, having discipline in life is all the more useful if I wish to accommodate other things, because I am more likely to meet my regular needs while doing other things if I have a higher degree of discipline. I think that is the issue here: the lack of self-discipline. In other words, the lack of the ability to control yourself and do things that are good for you.
Now comes the big question: How do I increase my self-discipline? A popular method in my childhood was to make a decision to do something and start off with lots of energy. For example, if I want to wake up early in the morning and go jogging, I would wake up and jump out of the bed so that I am starting off my goal with lots of energy. Another complimentary way is to make schedules. That way, I would know how much time I'm using up and how much free time I have left. In addition to that, I am more likely to do it because I have thought about doing it well in advance!
I have not been able to take control of life for so long and that makes me so disappointed at myself, but I can't keep doing that anymore. I'm going to start bring some more order and discipline into my life. And I'm going to do that one step at a time. I can't expect myself to create a schedule now and stick to it forever, but I can expect to stick to the schedule to a reasonable degree and make modifications that would make it more likely for me to follow the entire thing.
This was initially supposed to be a journal entry, but I wanted to write a blog post so that I take my effort to make changes in life a little more seriously. It scares me that I'm posting this online, but hey, what the hell? I'll go ahead! I'll make a schedule, try to stick to it and see if I really do make good changes in my life!
summary: I don't think I take proper care of myself. It's not that I have good intentions, but somehow those intentions do not seem to translate into actions. I want to eat healthy food 3 times a day, drink plenty of water everyday, take 2 baths a day, read news everyday, learn something new everyday, talk to my parents everyday and do many more things! But I look at my lifestyle and I look at the list of things I want to do above, and I think to myself "How in the world can I do all that in a day?
title: Taking Care of Yourself

content:
I was listening to Brian Armstrong being interviewed on the Lex Fridman's podcast when I heard him say the following (or something along those lines).
"Don't think too much, just try something. Only then will you be able to figure something out."
I have been intending to start a blog for quite some time now, but I was never able to finish a post. I've made multiple excuses for myself, saying that I don't have the time or that the post is not perfect (It needs to be perfect, right?). But after listening to Brian say that (although he meant it for startups), I decided to go ahead and write a blog post (cause why not?). I know I'm going to make some mistakes, and I hope that I do, because that would at least make me lose the fear of making mistakes. I need to make mistakes because that is how I learn, and the same goes for you.
I have a lot of things that I want to talk about, and sadly enough, I believe that don't have enough knowledge to properly talk about any of them. Maybe I'll bring up enough courage to talk about such topics soon, but for now, I'll stick to something I'm familiar with - my inner voice.
What do I mean by my inner voice? The voice that tells you to sleep when you wake up lazy early in the morning. The voice that tells you that you need to wake up immediately because you will be late for your meeting with your project guide otherwise. The voice that prevents you from asking a question in a class, because you're afraid of judgement from others in the class. The voice that prevents you from running on the streets naked because you're aware of the decorum you need to maintain in the society. It's not something your ear processes. In fact, it often doesn't have a voice itself. Then why do I call it voice? Because that's how I heard others call it, and I want you to understand what I'm saying.
I'd like to give it a term, so let's call it cheese (as in something you eat, yes). I believe everyone has cheese in their heads. It has the abiility to govern your behaviour more than you think, and I believe that cheese likes you to do things that you are comfortable with. That is, cheese wants you to stay in your comfort zone. That's good, because it saves you from lots of things in the society, but you don't start doing new things if you stay inside your comfort zone. So, every once in a while, you need to override the cheese inside your head and get out of your comfort zone. I'm doing it right now as I'm writing the blog, and I'm very scared, but I feel very good about it.
The thing that helped me override the cheese inside my head is my inner voice. Don't get me wrong, I'm not referring to cheese. I'm referring to the conscious voice inside your head that does have language. You use the power of language to manifest an inner voice that can speak to your brain, and if you are able to convince yourself and win the argument against the cheese inside your head, you have control. I'd like to call this the inner voice, because it is more like an actual voice than cheese is, because it's language has a structure (although, it doesn't really have a voice, my bad)
I'm getting late for my CS 725 class, so I'll go now. I'll post this of course, but I'm gonna keep this in my mind.
title: First Post

content:
Hi, I'm Prabhat. I'm a Master's student at C-MInDS IIT Bombay studying Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence. I like to read, write, think, watch anime, work too much, take long breaks, run away from comfort zones, think a lot before making a decision, make impulsive decisions, and that's just a few things I like to do.
The problem is that I love to do things, but I'm not very good at any of them. This blog is an attempt to rectify that problem at least by a little 👀. I believe that I will become a good writer someday!
I have loved Mathematics since my childhood, but more I study it, the more I realize how more beautiful it can get. I believe that humans have a certain way of thinking and one can find commonalities among seemingly distinct areas of human knowledge (say Psychology, history and cartography). I have a feeling that Mathematics is one domain that can be closely related to any other area, which would make it a powerful tool to equip. I believe I'm right, but at the end of the day, I'm just a child wishing for good things to happen 🤷‍♂️.
I love making these connections between concepts that one wouldn't generally make, and that's the focus of most of my blog posts. They are a ginormous mass of clutter that I hope you, the reader, can make sense out of.
That being said, I am also trying to write sane blog posts too. I decided to start a "Learn with me" series on Multi-Armed bandits, a topic that I started to learn quite recently.
This About me page is still under construction, I will include more information as I make up my mind about how much I should tell you. For now, check out my posts maybe?
summary:
Hi, I'm Prabhat. I'm a Master's student at C-MInDS IIT Bombay studying Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence. I like to read, write, think, watch anime, work too much, take long breaks, run away from comfort zones, think a lot before making a decision, make impulsive decisions, and that's just a few things I like to do.
The problem is that I love to do things, but I'm not very good at any of them.
title: About
